content: 2024–2025 state-of-the-art curiosity-driven active learning agents can use *epistemic value-weighted memory compression* to prioritize retaining only high-uncertainty experiences that drive future exploration, rather than compressing all long-term memories uniformly. This creates a self-reinforcing cycle where an agent’s long-term memory automatically prunes trivial experiences and amplifies the ambiguous, information-rich interactions that boost active learning performance.
source_topics: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
